mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered months or the signal loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in over 60% of cases or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during periods of low inflation reflects healthy capacity utilization without pricing pressure, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns fail to materialize in over 55% of low-inflation expansion months or the signal reverses direction during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising corporate earnings potential, which systematically pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month Rank IC remains below 0.01 or positive 5-day returns occur in less than 52% of months with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of months above the threshold or the predictive power vanishes when inflation expectations remain anchored below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates inventory accumulation and weakening consumer demand, which triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances or the interaction effect fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of high unemployment reflects severe demand destruction and idle capacity, which forces earnings downgrades and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered observations or the signal loses predictive accuracy when fiscal stimulus measures are announced within the same month.
